channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Photos: In this part of the world, nearly every chile pepper farmer is a woman
item.description: Chile peppers are a traditional part of Indian cuisine — and a key crop for women farmers. They say it's too demanding for men. "In spite of the challenges," says one, "we've found freedom."
item.content:encoded:
Chile peppers are a traditional part of Indian cuisine — and a key crop for women farmers. They say it's too demanding for men. "In spite of the challenges," says one, "we've found freedom."

(Image credit: Viraj Nayar for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Kamala Thiagarajan

item.title: U.S. negotiators prepare for more peace talks as Trump repeats threats to Iran
item.description: After Iran again closed the Strait of Hormuz, President Trump said the U.S. is returning to Pakistan for negotiations, but threatened to strike Iranian infrastructure if a deal isn't reached soon.
item.content:encoded:
After Iran again closed the Strait of Hormuz, President Trump said the U.S. is returning to Pakistan for negotiations, but threatened to strike Iranian infrastructure if a deal isn't reached soon.

(Image credit: Asghar Besharati)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Real estate investors are buying up long-term care facilities. Residents can suffer
item.description: Real estate investment trusts are landlords for thousands of nursing homes, assisted living facilities, and hospitals. Some select the managers and keep close watch but deny blame for bad care.
item.content:encoded:
Real estate investment trusts are landlords for thousands of nursing homes, assisted living facilities, and hospitals. Some select the managers and keep close watch but deny blame for bad care.

(Image credit: Taylor Glascock for KFF Health News)
item.dc:creator: Jordan Rau

item.title: This tariff-refund portal is about to be America's hottest website
item.description: Exactly two months after the Supreme Court struck down most of President Trump's tariffs, the U.S. government has set Monday as the day when some companies can begin requesting refunds.
item.content:encoded:
Exactly two months after the Supreme Court struck down most of President Trump's tariffs, the U.S. government has set Monday as the day when some companies can begin requesting refunds.

(Image credit: Nickolai Hammar)
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: She no longer remembers it's her birthday. He got her a present anyway
item.description: A special day can be tinged with sorrow when your partner has dementia. But then he found the perfect gift.
item.content:encoded: A special day can be tinged with sorrow when your partner has dementia. But then he found the perfect gift.
item.dc:creator: Marc Silver

item.title: School choice is booming in Iowa. Are students better off?
item.description: With school choice programs ascendant not just in Iowa but across the U.S., Cedar Rapids offers a preview of who wins and who loses when education meets the free market.
item.content:encoded:
With school choice programs ascendant not just in Iowa but across the U.S., Cedar Rapids offers a preview of who wins and who loses when education meets the free market.

(Image credit: Cliff Jette for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: The Little Probe That Could: Why Voyager 1 Matters, and Why NASA Just Switched Part of It Off
item.description: This week, NASA announced it had shut down one of that spacecraft's remaining science instruments — not because the mission has failed, but to keep it alive a little longer.
item.content:encoded:
This week, NASA announced it had shut down one of that spacecraft's remaining science instruments — not because the mission has failed, but to keep it alive a little longer.

(Image credit: NASA)
item.dc:creator: Willem Marx

item.title: North Korea launches ballistic missiles toward sea
item.description: South Korea's Joint Chiefs of Staff says the launches happened on Sunday morning from the North's eastern Sinpo area.
item.content:encoded:
South Korea's Joint Chiefs of Staff says the launches happened on Sunday morning from the North's eastern Sinpo area.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: US Coast Guard spots overturned vessel near Saipan during search for missing ship
item.description: The Mariana is a 145-foot dry cargo vessel registered in the U.S. It suffered engine failure Wednesday as a massive typhoon bore down on Saipan and nearby islands.
item.content:encoded:
The Mariana is a 145-foot dry cargo vessel registered in the U.S. It suffered engine failure Wednesday as a massive typhoon bore down on Saipan and nearby islands.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Life jacket worn by a passenger who survived the Titanic auctioned off for over $900,000
item.description: A life jacket worn by a passenger on the RMS Titanic has sold at auction for 670,000 pounds, which is more than $900,000.
item.content:encoded:
A life jacket worn by a passenger on the RMS Titanic has sold at auction for 670,000 pounds, which is more than $900,000.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
